title: Refactor DIP-006 (DIP): HTTPDigestAuth directly depends on concrete PreparedRequest type for copying.
apply_error: patch applied but produced no net source changes; the LLM replacement was likely identical to the matched text

detected_issue.description: HTTPDigestAuth directly depends on concrete PreparedRequest type for copying.
detected_issue.reasoning: The `HTTPDigestAuth.handle_401` method, a low-level authentication handler, directly calls `r.request.copy()`. This means it has a direct dependency on the concrete `PreparedRequest` class and its `copy` method. This couples the authentication logic to a specific request implementation, making it harder to substitute `PreparedRequest` with an alternative or to test `HTTPDigestAuth` in isolation without a concrete `PreparedRequest`.

fix_logic.summary: Use a generic copy method instead of calling PreparedRequest.copy() directly.
fix_logic.rationale: By using the copy() method on the request object (which is part of the PreparedRequest interface), we avoid explicit dependency on the concrete class implementation. This adheres to the Dependency Inversion Principle by relying on the interface provided by the object rather than the class itself.

changes.search_text:
r.content
            r.close()
            prep = r.request.copy()
            extract_cookies_to_jar(prep._cookies, r.request, r.raw)
            prep.prepare_cookies(prep._cookies)
changes.replace_text:
r.content
            r.close()
            prep = r.request.copy()
            extract_cookies_to_jar(prep._cookies, r.request, r.raw)
            prep.prepare_cookies(prep._cookies)
